perturbation.original_text: Either party, at its sole expense, upon at least thirty (30) days written notice (and not more than once in respect of any accounting period) will have the right to inspect the other party's books regarding the obligations hereunder for a period of two (2) years from the date on which any statement is rendered. Such auditing party must make specific written objection within such two (2) year period. Thereafter, it will be deemed to have consented to any such statements or accountings which will then be considered an account stated as between the parties, not subject to any objection for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party, at its sole expense, upon at least fifteen (15) days written notice (and not more than once in respect of any accounting period) will have the right to inspect the other party's books regarding the obligations hereunder for a period of two (2) years from the date on which any statement is rendered. Such auditing party must make specific written objection within such one (1) year period. Thereafter, it will be deemed to have consented to any such statements or accountings which will then be considered an account stated as between the parties, not subject to any objection for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the notice period required for inspecting the other party's books (changing it from thirty (30) days to fifteen (15) days). In addition it changes the period to file a written objection after an audit from two(2) years to one(1) year. This contradicts the requirement, outlined later in the section and creates uncertainty about the conditions governing such inspections and objections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accountings. Accounting statements and, if applicable, royalty payments for products sold will be rendered by each party quarterly within thirty (30) days after the expiration of each calendar quarter. Only products which have been paid or credited to the account of the seller shall be deemed sold. No royalties will be payable on the sales of any products by any third-party distributors or licensees until such time as accountings and payment or final credit therefor has been received by the paying party. Either party, at its sole expense, upon at least fifteen (15) days written notice (and not more than once in respect of any accounting period) will have the right to inspect the other party's books regarding the obligations hereunder for a period of two (2) years from the date on which any statement is rendered. Such auditing party must make specific written objection within such one (1) year period. Thereafter, it will be deemed to have consented to any such statements or accountings which will then be considered an account stated as between the parties, not subject to any objection for any reason whatsoever. Provided the objecting party has made timely written objection, as aforesaid, such party may file an action regarding same within two (2) years and six (6) months after the applicable statement is rendered, after which time any such action will be deemed barred.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original notice period from 'thirty (30) days' to 'fifteen (15) days' and the objection period from 'two (2) years' to 'one (1) year'. The contradicted text uses the changed text which now states 'fifteen (15) days written notice' and 'one (1) year period' to file an objection. Provided the objecting party has made timely written objection, as aforesaid, such party may file an action regarding same within two (2) years and six (6) months after the applicable statement is rendered, after which time any such action will be deemed barred'. This creates a contradiction because the objection period is now one year, while the period to file an action is two and a half years.

perturbation.original_text: The prevailing party in any such dispute arising hereunder will be entitled to recover from the other party its reasonable attorneys' fees in connection therewith in addition to the costs thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The prevailing party in any such dispute arising hereunder will not be entitled to recover from the other party its reasonable attorneys' fees in connection therewith in addition to the costs thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction. It states the prevailing party is not entitled to recover attorney fees, contradicting general legal principles and potentially discouraging parties from pursuing legitimate claims under the agreement. This introduces uncertainty about cost recovery in disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous a. This Agreement contains the entire understanding of the parties hereto relating to the subject matter hereof and cannot be changed or terminated except by an instrument signed by the party to be bound. A waiver by either party of any term or condition of this Agreement in any instance will not be deemed or construed as a waiver of such term or condition for the future, or of any subsequent breach thereof. All remedies, rights, undertakings, obligations, and agreements contained in this Agreement will be cumulative and none of them will be in limitation of any other remedy, right, undertaking, obligation or agreement of either party. The headings of the paragraphs hereof are for convenience only and will not be deemed to limit or in any way affect the scope, meaning or intent of this Agreement or any portion thereof. b. This Agreement has been entered into in the State of Tennessee, and the validity, interpretation and legal effect of this Agreement will be governed by the laws of the State of Tennessee applicable to contracts entered into and performed entirely within the State of Tennessee. The venue for any controversy or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement or breach thereof, shall be the appropriate state and federal courts located in Nashville, Tennessee. Accordingly, each party hereto consents and submits to the jurisdiction of such courts. The prevailing party in any such dispute arising hereunder will not be entitled to recover from the other party its reasonable attorneys' fees in connection therewith in addition to the costs thereof. c. If any part of this Agreement will be determined to be invalid or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction or by any other legally constituted body having jurisdiction to make such determination, the remainder of this Agreement will remain in full force and effect. d. EACH PARTY HAS BEEN REPRESENTED BY INDEPENDENT LEGAL COUNSEL OR HAS HAD THE UNRESTRICTED OPPORTUNITY TO BE REPRESENTED BY INDEPENDENT LEGAL COUNSEL OF ITS CHOICE FOR PURPOSES OF ADVISING IT IN CONNECTION WITH THE NEGOTIATION AND EXECUTION OF THIS AGREEMENT. IF EITHER PARTY HAS NOT BEEN
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the prevailing party would be entitled to recover attorney's fees. The changed text explicitly states that the prevailing party will *not* be entitled to recover attorney's fees. The contradicted text is the exact same sentence appearing later in the document. This creates a direct, in-text contradiction on a key point (attorney's fees recovery), which falls under the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. The change alters an entitlement, creating a clear conflict that would lead to uncertainty and potential legal disputes. This directly violates the principle of contract consistency within the document.
